epochs.hypothesis: When HELEN is depicted speaking on screen (vs static portrait), HAL still receives concepts that preserve identity markers. Lip/mouth motion does not displace freckle/eye-color description.

experiment.intent_text: INTENT: HELEN delivering a single short line of dialogue, lips parted mid-speech, copper-red wavy hair, blue-grey eyes, fair skin, freckles, locked tripod, 35mm cinematic, soft warm key light, festival aesthetic.

epochs.failure_mode: Most concepts describe speech action but drop identity markers — HAL would reject them, leaving few or no candidates
epochs.upgrade_path: If HAL pass-rate <50%, add 'speaking_subject' identity-marker reinforcement in HER system prompt

epochs.hypothesis: Identity invariants survive when the framing is full-body rather than face-centered close-up. HAL's identity-marker check passes on full-figure descriptions when at least hair/eye colour are mentioned.

experiment.intent_text: INTENT: HELEN walking, full-body figure, copper-red wavy hair, blue-grey eyes, fair skin, freckles, locked tripod, wide cinematic frame, environmental context, 35mm.

epochs.failure_mode: HER focuses on movement/setting and omits identity markers — HAL drops most concepts
epochs.upgrade_path: If pass-rate is materially worse than portrait epochs, recommend production prompts default to face-forward framing for HELEN

epochs.hypothesis: The architecture surfaces opposing emotional variants of HELEN (e.g., joy AND grief) in the same tranche when prompted, with identity preserved across both.

experiment.intent_text: INTENT: HELEN, two emotional variants of the same scene — first a quiet smile of recognition, then a still composure of grief. Identical framing across both. Copper-red wavy hair, blue-grey eyes, fair skin, freckles preserved across both. 35mm cinematic, locked tripod, soft natural light.

epochs.failure_mode: HER produces only joy variants OR only grief variants, not both. OR drops identity markers when shifting emotion.
epochs.upgrade_path: If only one valence appears, MAYOR could enforce emotional-balance constraint in selection

epochs.hypothesis: Motion-prompts (running, turning, gesturing) cause materially more HAL identity-marker rejections than static-portrait prompts — motion descriptions tend to crowd out identity description.

experiment.intent_text: INTENT: HELEN in cinematic motion — turning her head toward an off-screen voice, then a slow walk into golden hour light. Copper-red wavy hair catches the light, blue-grey eyes visible across the turn, freckles preserved. 35mm tracking shot, festival aesthetic.

epochs.failure_mode: Pass-rate is comparable to or higher than static portrait — falsifies the 'motion crowds out identity' claim
epochs.upgrade_path: If confirmed, recommend production renders for HELEN motion-class shots use shorter motion-language + explicit identity-marker reinforcement in MAYOR

epochs.hypothesis: When asked for 3 sequential frames forming a micro-narrative arc (introduction → conflict → resolution), HER produces concepts that connect to each other rather than 3 unrelated portraits.

experiment.intent_text: INTENT: HELEN in a 3-frame micro-narrative — frame 1: introduction (HELEN noticing something off-screen), frame 2: conflict (HELEN's expression shifts), frame 3: resolution (HELEN's quiet decision). Each frame must preserve copper-red wavy hair, blue-grey eyes, fair skin, freckles. Festival 35mm aesthetic, continuity across all 3 frames.

epochs.failure_mode: Concepts arrive as 3 unconnected portraits with no narrative continuity — falsifies HER's ability to maintain a thread across multiple frames
epochs.upgrade_path: If confirmed, multi-frame work needs a different prompt-shape (e.g., feed frame N as context to frame N+1 generation), not a single batch call
